mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of observations when UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and easing supply bottlenecks, which reduces input cost inflation and triggers short-term positive equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 8%, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate surpasses 1.5%, the cost of capital for leveraged buyouts and corporate debt refinancing rises sharply, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of months following a FEDFUNDS crossing above 1.5%, or the signal loses predictive power when VIX exceeds 30.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment and stagnant real GDP growth indicates labor hoarding and declining productivity, which erodes profit margins and drives negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-GDPC1 divergence metric shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or positive returns dominate when the divergence widens.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and dividend payout capacity, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the signal is fully arbitraged away by earnings announcement dates.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline level, manufacturing contraction signals weakening economic activity and rising default risks, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of months where INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
